name: वेद
description: The oldest and most authoritative scriptures of Hinduism, considered Shruti (divine revelation).

name: उपनिषद
description: Philosophical texts that form the concluding part of the Vedas, exploring the nature of Brahman and Atman.

name: पुराण
description: Ancient texts narrating the history of the universe, genealogies of gods, and stories of kings and sages.

name: महाकाव्य
description: The great narrative poems — Ramayana and Mahabharata — that form the foundation of Hindu culture.

name: गीता
description: Sacred songs and dialogues conveying spiritual wisdom, including the Bhagavad Gita.

name: स्तोत्र
description: Hymns of praise and devotion composed by saints and sages to various deities.

name: स्मृति
description: Texts based on remembered tradition, including law codes and ethical guidelines.

name: सूत्र
description: Aphoristic texts distilling complex philosophical teachings into concise formulas.

name: तन्त्र
description: Esoteric texts containing rituals, meditation practices, and philosophical teachings.

name: आगम
description: Texts prescribing temple rituals, yoga practices, and philosophical doctrines.

name: आरती
description: Devotional songs sung during the ritual of waving a lamp before a deity, expressing love and gratitude.

name: अभंग
description: Devotional poetry in Marathi composed by the Varkari saints of Maharashtra, expressing longing for Vitthal.

name: दोहा
description: Couplets of spiritual wisdom and moral teachings composed by saints like Kabir and Ramdas.

name: नीति
description: Texts on statecraft, ethics, and practical wisdom — including Chanakya Niti and Vidura Niti.

name: संत साहित्य
description: Literature of the Bhakti movement saints of Maharashtra, including the teachings and compositions of the Varkari and other traditions.
